master_row.title: Trawinistic Laplacian
master_row.canonical_statement: The Trawinistic Laplacian extends the usual second-order diffusion operator to the singularly regularized Trawinistic manifold. It applies the Trawinistic connection twice while raising indices with the regularized inverse metric.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a differential equation relating ∇, φ, a, g_, b.
master_row.dictionary: For a scalar field (), the Trawinistic Laplacian is defined by (^T=^T_a^Ta), where (^T) is the Trawinistic connection and indices are raised using the regularised inverse metric.
master_row.encyclopedia: $_T$ is the Laplacian adapted to TZP geometry. It measures second-order field variation using the Trawinistic connection. Interpretive role: This operator governs diffusion, smoothing, and spectral decomposition once the singular manifold has been given a consistent connection. It is the natural second-order scalar operator for the TRAWIN-normalized geometry.
